label: Temple of the Sealed Oath
description: The Temple of the Sealed Oath occupies a fortified precinct of salvaged ashlar bound with newer brick, its façade cut with narrow lancet slits and capped by a squat bell cote rather than an open tower. The interior centers on a stone dais encircled by iron-bound oath rails, with wall tablets of codified vows set into mortar like civic decrees. Shuttered alcoves and screened side chapels preserve doctrinal texts under seal, mirroring Varric Holdfast’s regime of licensed records and contested orthodoxy. Hazard placards in formal script line the vestibule, tying ritual practice to the city’s interdiction order and the memory of breach-sites beyond the gates.
